3.4.9 kosambiyasuttaṁ -.summary: This is the title of a sutta, named after the town of Kosambī where it was delivered.

breakdown: Intensifying prefix; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.

abbhatthaṁ.gram: neuter noun (from attha), often adverbial accusative
breakdown: Prefix "towards, over"; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.
Setting, disappearance (as of the sun); home, end.

abbhokāsagatassa.meaning: gone into the open, staying in the open air
abbhokāsagatassa.summary: This is a compound meaning "gone (gata) to the open space (abbhokāsa)".
breakdown: Open air, open space, the open sky.
Gone, gone to (past participle of gacchati).

abbhokāse.meaning: in the open air, under the open sky
abbhokāse.gram: masculine noun, locative singular of abbhokāsa
breakdown: Prefix; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.
Space, open space, opportunity (ava + √kās "to shine, appear").

abbhokāso.meaning: open air, open sky; open space
breakdown: Prefix; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.
abbhokāso.note: divergence: the lexicon's breakdown gives abbha ("sky, cloud") + okāsa ("space"); DPD derives it as abhi + ava + √kās. Both analyses circulate; the sense "open air, open sky" is the same.

abbhuggacchati.meaning: to go forth, go out; to rise into (the air); to spread abroad
breakdown: Prefix "towards, over"; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.
Prefix "towards, over"; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.

abbhuggata.meaning: gone forth, gone out, risen; spread abroad
breakdown: Prefix "towards, over"; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.
Prefix "towards"; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.

abbhāgata.meaning: having arrived, having come; a guest, a stranger
abbhāgata.gram: adjective/past participle; as a noun, masculine
breakdown: Prefix "towards"; before a vowel it becomes abbh-.

abbuhe.summary: A form of the verb abbuhati/abbahati "to pull out"; the related gerund is abbuyha "having drawn out".
breakdown: Prefix "to, towards" (doubled consonant by sandhi: abb-).

abbuhe/abbuyha.meaning: to pull out, draw out; (abbuyha) having drawn out
abbuhe/abbuyha.gram: verb forms of abbuhati/abbahati; abbuyha is the gerund
breakdown: Prefix "to, towards" (doubled consonant by sandhi: abb-).

abhabbaṁ.gram: adjective (potential participle of na bhavati), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Able, capable, possible (potential participle of bhavati, √bhū).

abhabbo.meaning: incapable, unable; one for whom something is impossible
abhabbo.gram: adjective (potential participle of na bhavati), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Able, capable, possible (potential participle of bhavati, √bhū).

abhabbābhāsaṁ.meaning: having the appearance of being incapable; seeming incapable
abhabbābhāsaṁ.summary: This is a compound meaning "the appearance (ābhāsa) of one incapable (abhabba)".

abhavissa.meaning: he would be, it would have been
abhavissa.gram: verb, 3rd person singular conditional of bhavati (√bhū)
breakdown: Present stem of bhavati "to be, become" (√bhū).

abhavissaṁsu.meaning: they would be, they would have been
abhavissaṁsu.gram: verb, 3rd person plural conditional of bhavati (√bhū)
breakdown: Present stem of bhavati "to be, become" (√bhū).

abhi.meaning: towards, about, against; over, fully (intensifying prefix)

abhibhosmi.meaning: I am the conqueror, I am the one who has overcome
abhibhosmi.summary: A sandhi fusion of abhibhū "conqueror, master" and asmi "I am".
abhibhosmi.gram: sandhi compound: masculine noun + verb, 1st person singular present
breakdown: "I am", 1st person singular of atthi. (The fusion ū + a → o.)

abhibhūto.gram: past participle of abhibhavati, masculine nominative singular

breakdown: Suffix of the future passive participle, "to be ...-ed".
Root "to think, brood"; abhijjhā is literally "brooding over" what others have.
abhijjhā.goenka: Abhijjhā, covetousness or greed, is listed among the ten unwholesome actions (akusala kamma): in the Sammādiṭṭhi-sutta it stands alongside ill will and wrong view as one of the unwholesome states a Noble Disciple learns to understand and abandon. Its opposite, anabhijjhā (freedom from covetousness), is correspondingly wholesome. In the satipaṭṭhāna context the meditator practises "having removed abhijjhā and domanassa (covetousness and aversion) with regard to the world".

abhijjhādomanassaṁ.meaning: covetousness and discontent; craving and aversion
abhijjhādomanassaṁ.summary: A compound pairing the two poles of mental reaction: abhijjhā (covetousness) and domanassa (discontent, distress).
abhijjhādomanassaṁ.goenka: This pair appears in the refrain of the Satipaṭṭhāna-sutta: the meditator dwells observing body, sensations, mind and mental contents "vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ" — having removed covetousness and discontent with regard to the world. In the tradition's understanding these are the two basic reactions of craving and aversion that the practice of equanimous observation dissolves.

abhikkantavaṇṇā.meaning: of surpassing beauty, of excellent appearance
abhikkantavaṇṇā.summary: A compound describing devas: abhikkanta "surpassing, excellent" + vaṇṇa "beauty, complexion".

abhikkantaṁ.meaning: excellent, supreme; wonderful (as an exclamation of approval)
abhikkantaṁ.gram: past participle of abhikkamati, neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Root "to step, advance" + past participle suffix -ta (with kk doubling).

abhikkantāya.meaning: advanced, far gone (of the night: "when the night was far advanced")
abhikkantāya.gram: past participle of abhikkamati, feminine locative/instrumental singular
breakdown: Root "to step, advance" + past participle suffix -ta.

abhinibbatti.meaning: birth, rebirth, becoming; arising into existence

abhinikkhamanato.meaning: from the going forth; from retiring from the world to devote oneself to the ascetic life
abhinikkhamanato.gram: neuter action noun with ablative suffix -to

abhinippīḷayato.meaning: crushing, subduing; of one who crushes
abhinippīḷayato.gram: present participle of abhinippīḷayati, genitive/dative singular

abhiniropanā.meaning: firm planting (of the mind); mental fixity; application of the mind, fixing one's mind

abhinivajjetvā.meaning: having avoided, having got rid of

abhinivajjeyyāsi.meaning: you should get rid of, you should avoid, turn back from, desist
abhinivajjeyyāsi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular optative of abhinivajjeti

abhinivissa.meaning: having adhered to, having clung to

abhinīto.meaning: brought to, led to; obliged by
abhinīto.gram: past participle of abhineti, masculine nominative singular

abhisajjati.meaning: to be angry, to scold; to take offence
breakdown: Root "to cling, stick"; with abhi-, "to get stuck on, take offence, be angry".

abhisambujjhati.meaning: to understand fully, to become fully enlightened

abhisamecca.meaning: having attained, having fully realized; would attain
breakdown: Root "to go"; abhisameti = "to attain, realize" + gerund suffix -tya (giving -ecca).

abhisameti.meaning: to realize, to comprehend, to attain
breakdown: Root "to go"; with the prefixes, "to come at, realize fully".

abhisamparāyañca.meaning: and the next existence; and the state after death
abhisamparāyañca.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular + conjunction ca
breakdown: The next world, the state after death (saṁ + parā + √i).
= ca "and", with sandhi ṁ + c → ñc.

abhisamāgacchati.meaning: to come to understand completely, to grasp fully
abhisamāgacchati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present (abhi + saṁ + ā + √gam)

abhisantāpayato.meaning: tormenting, afflicting, constraining; of one who torments
abhisantāpayato.gram: present participle of abhisantāpayati (causative), genitive/dative singular
breakdown: Root "to burn, heat"; causative stem tāpaya- "to cause to burn, torment".

abhivadanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of abhivadati (abhi + √vad)
